key_insight: FastDSAC improves scalable humanoid locomotion by constraining exploration with a truncated Gaussian policy, preserving policy plasticity while stabilizing value learning under high update-to-data ratios.
engineering_relevance: The method is useful for fast, parallelized robot training where sample efficiency, training stability, and reliable convergence are critical for deploying locomotion controllers.
method: Use a truncated Gaussian to block out-of-distribution actions while keeping exploration stochastic.
Apply constrained exploration as an implicit regularizer to reduce policy plasticity loss from aggressive updates.
Maintain continuous action modeling with adaptive variance regulation for more accurate value estimation.
evidence_signal: Experiments on MuJoCo Playground and HumanoidBench show more stable training, faster early convergence, and stronger asymptotic performance than state-of-the-art baselines.
watch_item: The truncation and variance-control settings may need careful tuning to avoid overly restricting exploration or biasing action sampling.
paper_title: FastDSAC: Enhancing Policy Plasticity via Constrained Exploration for Scalable Humanoid Locomotion
